channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Netskope extends data security with Dasera acquisition
item.description: Netskope today announced it has broadened its data protection capabilities by integrating data security posture management (DSPM) technology into its Netskope One Platform via its acquisition of Dasera.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year. Terms of the settlement were not disclosed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missed out on the northern lights? Scientists expect more solar storms to produce auroras
item.description: Space weather experts say expect to see more northern lights in unusual places as the sun continues to sizzle.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Web Domain Revenue Boom: Small Countries Cash In
item.description: Small countries are generating significant revenue from unique web domains, driven by growing demand for distinctive address endings
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rare boss Craig Duncan to take over as Xbox Game Studios' head in November
item.description: Craig Duncan, head of Sea of Thieves studio Rare, will take over as the Xbox Game Studios boss later this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to transform scientific discoveries into better treatments
item.description: As I reflect on how we can more effectively bring treatments to patients, one central question emerges: How can we transform scientific discoveries into better treatments? The intersection of science and innovation is rapidly evolving, yet translating cutting-edge research into impactful therapies r
item.dc:creator: Guadalupe Hayes-Mota, Fast Company

item.title: How to choose a flexible unified SASE solution
item.description: SASE is at the top of all CIOs’ minds, and for good reason. When deployed properly, SASE, which combines SD-WAN with cloud-delivered security via SSE, enables all users and devices, regardless of location, to securely access an organization’s hybrid network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Six steps to creating a successful IT strategy: A guide for CIOs
item.description: In today’s rapidly evolving technological landscape, the role of the CIO has transcended simply managing IT infrastructure to becoming a pivotal player in enabling business strategy.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: FBI Visits Independent Journalist Over Published Dossier: A Threat to Democracy?
item.description: FBI visits journalist Ken Klippenstein over published dossier, raising concerns about censorship and national security threats
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Cato Networks extends SASE platform with digital experience monitoring
item.description: Cato Networks today introduced digital experience monitoring (DEM) capabilities that will augment the company’s cloud SASE platform with application traffic and network performance data that IT operations teams need to optimize end-user experiences.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 5 tips for getting the most out of YouTube TV
item.description: YouTube TV has slowly but surely emerged as one of the better live TV providers for those of us who are tired of renting cable boxes or managing satellite dishes.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: How Avaya helped Leon County rapidly deploy a contact center during a state of emergency
item.description: In May 2024, Leon County, Florida faced many significant challenges when three tornadoes, including two F2s and one F1, struck the area causing extensive damage. This natural disaster resulted in 725 severely damaged structures, 175 of which were destroyed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Ernst & Young’s AI platform is ‘radically’ reshaping operations
item.description: Multinational consultancy Ernst & Young (EY) said generative AI (genAI) is “radically reshaping” the way it operates, and the company boasts a 96% adoption rate of the technology by employees. After spending $1.4 billion on a customized generative AI platform called EY.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Case for Reshaping Traditional Industries with Tech
item.description: Digitalization is everywhere. Be it in the energy industry, e-government services, manufacturing, or logistics, the fourth industrial revolution is having a profound impact. Thanks to AI, 5G-A, cloud, and other technologies, the physical world is merging with the digital world.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Innovación y mejora continua en la granja: la apuesta por la excelencia operativa de Costa Food Group
item.description: El Grupo Empresarial Costa vio la luz en 1966 en la localidad oscense de Fraga. Desde entonces, su crecimiento y expansión han sido envidiables, llegando a traspasar fronteras y afincándose en más de un centenar de países.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The Internet Archive is back online after a cyberattack
item.description: The Internet Archive is back online after new of a cyberattack took out its digital library and popular Wayback Machine on October 9.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Adobe aims to teach AI, online content skills to 30 million
item.description: A new program from Adobe aims to teach 30 million people AI, content creation, and marketing skills by 2030. It’s an extension of the Adobe Digital Academy, which already trains people for new careers in fields like digital marketing, user experience design, and data science.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: HUD Seeks to Bridge AI Skills Gap through Innovative Assessment Approach
item.description: The Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) is pioneering a new way to identify and address the skills gap in artificial intelligence, utilizing a competency model and personalized training plans for its employees.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple accused of restricting workers' Slack, social media use
item.description: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) filed a complaint against Apple on Thursday, accusing the iPhone maker of improperly restricting workers’ use of Slack and other social media.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: What in the world is going on at Ubisoft?
item.description: Ubisoft is no stranger to takeover whispers. In 2004, Electronic Arts made a run at the video game publisher, but was ultimately rebuffed. In 2015, Vivendi made an even more serious attempt that took years to defuse.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Murder trial of East Bay tech consultant in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee begins
item.description: Lee’s death stunned the tech community, and fellow executives and engineers penned tributes to his generosity and brilliance. Lee was chief product officer of cryptocurrency platform MobileCoin when he died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: 50 well-preserved Viking Age skeletons unearthed in Denmark
item.description: Archaeologists in Denmark have unearthed a Viking Age burial ground containing some 50 “exceptionally well-preserved” skeletons.
item.dc:creator: James Brooks, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Excel basics: Get started with charts and sparklines
item.description: Microsoft Excel offers a plethora of tools for representing your data visually. The most basic of these — and arguably the most useful — is the humble chart. But it’s not always easy to know where to begin with charts. We’re here to help.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How schools are tackling misinformation as false claims about hurricanes, election run wild
item.description: Schools face challenges teaching children how to identify and handle misinformation without getting political, especially in recent days as false claims swirl surrounding the recent hurricanes and the upcoming election.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Fake Jennifer Aniston bikini body ad
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AMD boosts AI performance in new line of enterprise PC chips
item.description: AMD’s big Advancing AI event in San Francisco on Thursday underlined how quickly the microprocessor industry has pivoted to artificial intelligence (AI) as its main sales pitch.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: This privacy and compliance threat throws shade at iPhone Mirroring
item.description: Apple-focused IT admins using device management software should perhaps temporarily disable iPhone Mirroring across their device fleets to prevent inadvertent privacy or compliance challenges as a result of using the new macOS Sequoia feature. (Apple is supposed to be working on a fix already.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Deno 2.0 arrives, ready to battle Node.js
item.description: Deno 2.0, a major update to the open source Deno runtime for JavaScript, TypeScript, and WebAssembly, is now available as a production release. It emphasizes backward compatibility with the rival Node.js runtime and NPM and a stabilized standard library.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation
